{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Alekhine's Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | |nd|pl| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} == Alekhine's Defence - Normal Variation == White's pawn is now weak. It should be reinforced with other pawns. The most intuitive move is [[/3. d4/]]. But White can also keep on attacking the knight and play [[/3. c4/]] delaying d4. An option may allow White to avoid the sharpest lines of the Alekhine : [[/3. Nc3/]]. In compensation for doubled pawns, White can accelerate their development after 3...Nxc3 4.dxc3, or strengthen their center and grab extra space with 4.bxc3. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}. '1.e4 Nf6 2.e5 Nd5'
